title: rootless containers non root workloads can't write to /dev/stdout nor /dev/stderr
body:
### Issue Description

I set out to moving all my containers to use the now integrated quadlet by creating ~/.config/containers/systemd entries for them.

Quadlet currently generates the containers to run with `--log-driver passthrough` and no `--tty`, which AFAIU is great as we then get things straight to the journal without copies. The problem arises when the main process inside the container does not run as root. In such case, the main process will fail trying to write to /dev/stdout or /dev/stderr.

A good example of this is the nextcloud container with its Apache2 running as the www-data user (33) and its logging configured to put the access log to /dev/stdout and its error log to /dev/stderr

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Write a user systemd service named rootless-root.service that has an ExecStart running the following
```
/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --log-driver passthrough --runtime /usr/bin/crun --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon --entrypoint /bin/bash docker.io/library/nextcloud:25.0.4 -c 'echo "FOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO"; ls -l /dev; ls -l /proc/self/fd'
```
2. Write a systemd service named rootless-regularuser.service one that does the same but as the `www-data` user:
```
/usr/bin/podman run --name=systemd-%N --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --log-driver passthrough --runtime /usr/bin/crun --cgroups=split --sdnotify=conmon --user 33 --entrypoint /bin/bash docker.io/library/nextcloud:25.0.4 -c 'echo "FOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO"; ls -l /dev; ls -l /proc/self/fd'
```
3. systemctl --user start rootless-root.service
4. systemctl --user start rootless-regularuser.service


### Describe the results you received

Only the first one has any output in the journal:

### Describe the results you expected

rootless-root.service and rootless-regularuser.service should have had the same content in the journal

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

My impression is that the the fact that /proc/self/fd/* is only writeable by root causes the issue:
```
Mar 04 01:42:51 nausicaa nextcloud[631321]: lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Mar  4 00:42 0 -> /dev/null
Mar 04 01:42:51 nausicaa nextcloud[631321]: lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Mar  4 00:42 1 -> socket:[8478587]
Mar 04 01:42:51 nausicaa nextcloud[631321]: lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Mar  4 00:42 2 -> socket:[8478587]
Mar 04 01:42:51 nausicaa nextcloud[631321]: lr-x------. 1 root root 64 Mar  4 00:42 3 -> /proc/1/fd
```

I tried setting them to be world writeable by doing chmod a+rw inside the container as root before dropping privileges 

but got:
```
type=AVC msg=audit(1677892706.383:2117): avc:  denied  { setattr } for  pid=634981 comm="chmod" name="UNIX-STREAM" dev="sockfs" ino=8499252 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c743,c977 tcontext=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023 tclass=unix_stream_socket permissive=0
	Was caused by:
		Missing type enforcement (TE) allow rule.

		You can use audit2allow to generate a loadable module to allow this access.
```
in audit2allow

comments.body:
changing the log driver to the older `journald` setting does not exhibit this issue. Unfortunately quadlet is not exposing the logging driver as something one can set. However, one can do:
```
PodmanArgs=--log-driver journald
```
Quadlet will generate the admittedly ugly ExecStart with two different --log-driver options but it thankfully keeps the last one, which is the one set by PodmanArgs.
I see that #17588 will allow the user to set the logdriver in quadlet

Thanks for opening this issue.

As you've noted, Podman 4.5 will include the Quadlet Key LogDriver to allow the user to change the value.

Please note that a similar issue is being discussed here #17572. While the issue is Closed, there is still a discussion about whether Quadlet should not set a default value at all.
Apart from using the journald log driver, would it be possible to make passthrough work for non root users by setting up the socket differently?

> Apart from using the journald log driver, would it be possible to make passthrough work for non root users by setting up the socket differently?

@Luap99 @vrothberg @rhatdan WDYT?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I don't think we can chmod the journald socket fds.
If `passthrough` does not work in these cases it is not a suitable default for quadlet IMO.

> I don't think we can chmod the journald socket fds. If `passthrough` does not work in these cases it is not a suitable default for quadlet IMO.

I agree.  I've been voting to change the default log-driver of Quadlet already.  It's just too hard to debug what's wrong.
So we just use the systemd default from containers.conf?

> So we just use the systemd default from containers.conf?

Yes.  RHIVOS could set it to `passthrough`, for instance.
